prose:
**Fukui reactivity analysis of benzaldehyde** — DFT, tier `standard` (**B3LYP/def2-TZVP**, PySCF; density fitting on by default), **gas phase** (no implicit solvent), charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry: user-supplied `.xyz` (/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/benzaldehyde/benzaldehyde.xyz, 14 atoms), used as-is (single-point property evaluation; not re-optimized at this level). Partial charges computed with the Mulliken (PySCF) scheme. The N±1 finite-difference states used the cation (charge +1, multiplicity 2) and anion (charge −1, multiplicity 2), which are open-shell (UKS) doublets.

**Property computed:** condensed-to-atom Fukui functions (f⁺ nucleophilic-attack susceptibility / electrophilicity, f⁻ electrophilic-attack susceptibility / nucleophilicity, f⁰, and the dual descriptor) derived from Mulliken charges of the neutral, cation, and anion.

**Headline result — most electrophilic site:** atom index 3 (1-based), a ring carbon (C), with **f⁺ = 0.39938961** (dimensionless). The most nucleophilic site is atom 1 (the carbonyl O) with f⁻ = 0.23839906.

**Backend:** PySCF (DFT/UKS). 

**Integrity / convergence:** the engine reports `integrity.status = ok`, `trustworthy = true`. All three integrity checks passed (`ok: true`). I reproduce every check message verbatim below as warnings, since each is labeled severity `warning`:

- schema_header [ok]: "common header present"

Both charge-conservation sums equal 1.0000, consistent with well-converged N±1 states.

**Method note:** Condensed Fukui functions inherit the well-known basis- and scheme-dependence of Mulliken population analysis; the specific numeric values should be read as relative reactivity indices at this level of theory rather than as basis-set-converged quantities.

**Reproducibility.** Exact command: `/home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --tier standard --charge 0 --mult 1 /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/benzaldehyde/benzaldehyde.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpazac57v9/live.json`.
